qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Land 4 tmpfs-pressure deliverables (L1 SessionStart banner, L1.5 UserPromptSubmit pressure hook, L2 cron sweep with EXCLUSION-FIRST + IEC-i freed_total + per-category logging, L3 convention note) so the dev box no longer ENOSPCs from accumulated /tmp scratch.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex finding #3: worst-case runtime of L1.5 hook (~16s when both mounts pressured: read 1s + df 2s + flock 1s + df 2s + 2x du 5s) can exceed the 15s settings timeout. This could truncate du output AND consume a rate-limit slot in the same prompt. Mitigation already in place: hook is non-blocking by contract (exit 0); next prompt re-emits at full fidelity if pressure persists. In-scope-minor; not blocking. Could be addressed in follow-up by tightening per-du to 4s.

Codex finding #4: `find -mtime +N` semantics mean strictly more than N full 24h periods, so >3d L3 wording is loosely accurate (actual minimum age is 72-96h depending on the daily cron landing). Spec uses >3d / >7d shorthand throughout; behavior is consistent with spec wording. Documentation precision improvement; not a functional bug.

Codex finding #1: rate-limit state file lives on the pressured filesystem (/tmp). Spec explicitly mandates this path (AC3 'Counter file: /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-<sanitized_token>' and AC4 'EXTENDED with claude-pressure-warn-* per M3' — the L2 sweep at >7d covers it). Moving to /var/tmp would change a spec-mandated path. The hook is non-blocking and exits 0 unconditionally even if lock-open fails under ENOSPC — best-effort visibility is the contract. Spec wins per codex Rule 4.

Codex finding #2: long-lived sessions (>7d) with old warning state but no current pressure can have counters swept by the >7d hook-state sweep, resetting the ≤3/session guarantee. Dev report explicitly classified this as F3 partial-scope decision in iteration-1: 'the silent-during-pressure-drop case for 1<=n<=2 is out_of_scope (the cleanup design explicitly sweeps stale state at >7d; widening to refresh on every prompt regardless of pressure would change spec design)'. Saturated-counter (n>=3) IS handled by mtime refresh per spec F7. Spec wins per codex Rule 4.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: L1 banner emits per-mount df with WARN prefix; L1.5 emits rate-limited pressure warning with top-5 du blocks per pressured mount; L2 sweeps broader patterns including new claude-pressure-warn-* state and *-scratch-[0-9]* convention; L3 documents convention. All 4 deliverables target the user's verbatim complaint (ENOSPC prevention via visibility + cleanup).

qa.overall_assessment: All 8 ACs pass. L1 banner emits 2 lines (no WARN at current 46%/64%). L2 dry-run shows 0 hard-exclusion path candidates AND 86 carve-out /tmp/claude-0/*.output candidates AND IEC-i freed_total=0B (dry-run). Settings.json preserves 5 SessionStart + 6th appended, 3 UserPromptSubmit + 4th appended, hook-level timeouts present (5/15s). Cron unchanged. L3 doc has all 4 required claims. Prior QA's empirical counter-file evidence (3/1,1,1,1/3 series) confirms AC3 rate-limit + per-session isolation. Codex returned 4 findings, all classified out_of_scope (3) or in_scope_minor (1) per codex Rule 3 — none blocking.

success_criteria_results.criterion: SessionStart df banner present (one compact line per mount)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Direct invocation of /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/hooks/session-tmpfs-banner.sh; captured stdout and exit code; verified line count and column shape.
success_criteria_results.details: Hook emits exactly 2 lines from `df -h /tmp /dev/shm | tail -n +2`. Exit 0.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Output captured this run: line 1 = `tmpfs           4.0G  1.9G  2.2G  46% /tmp`, line 2 = `tmpfs            16G  9.8G  5.6G  64% /dev/shm`. Both mounts include path and Use%. Exit code 0.

success_criteria_results.verification_method: Code-shape inspection of session-tmpfs-banner.sh:32-34 confirming `printf 'WARN %s\n' "$line"` is applied per-line at THRESHOLD=75 (line 15). Prior QA's empirical PATH-shim test (dev_report.self_verification.tests_run[0]) confirmed WARN appears per-line at >75% and absent at ≤75%.
success_criteria_results.details: Code path: `if [ "$pct" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]; then printf 'WARN %s\n' ...` — emits literal `WARN ` (5-byte, trailing space) per-line. At current 46%/64% no WARN appears (verified empirically this run).
success_criteria_results.evidence: session-tmpfs-banner.sh:32-34. Current invocation output contains no WARN literal (both mounts ≤75%); dev_report tests_run[0] documents PATH-shim verification.

success_criteria_results.verification_method: Code-shape inspection of userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh + counter-file evidence from prior QA's empirical tests left in /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-*.
success_criteria_results.details: Code shape: flock fd 9 opened INSIDE command substitution via brace-group `decision=$( { flock -x -w 1 9 || { printf 'skip'; exit 0; }; ...; } 9> "$LOCK_FILE" )` (lines 99-122). Parent shell branches: `[ "$decision" = "emit" ] || exit 0` (line 124). Saturated-counter branch (n>=3) refreshes mtime via `printf '%s\n' "$n" > "$COUNTER_FILE"` (line 115). Token derivation: stdin JSON session_id → CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env → sha256(PPID+proc_start) (lines 39-55). Sanitization to [A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64} with hash fallback (lines 58-61). Top-5 du block per pressured mount (lines 131-144). df/du wrapped in `timeout 2s` / `timeout 5s` (lines 72, 130, 139). PATH-resolved bare commands (no /usr/bin/ prefix). Empirical evidence: /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-qa-press-rl-1779220814 = 3 (same SID 4x → saturated), /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-qa-press-distinct-...-iter[1-4] = 1,1,1,1 (distinct SIDs → per-session isolation), /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-codex-post-fix-rl = 3 (post-fix retest saturated).
success_criteria_results.evidence: userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh:28-147 reviewed. /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-qa-press-rl-1779220814 = 3 (4 same-SID invocations capped at 3). iter1..iter4 = 1,1,1,1 (4 distinct SIDs each get a fresh slot). codex-post-fix-rl = 3 (post-codex-fix retest). Held-lock test exits in ~1s per dev_report tests_run[4].

success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh; verified shared EXCLUDE_PRUNE array (lines 68-75); verified run_tmp helper applies EXCLUDE_PRUNE at top of every /tmp find (line 99); verified action lives INSIDE non-prune branch via `-o \( "$@" -print \)` (line 99); verified mktemp -p /var/tmp for candidate temp file (line 106); verified tee-to-both-stdout-AND-log for dry-run (line 49); verified all required patterns present in dev/test scratch tier (lines 180-200) including `*-scratch-[0-9]*` (line 200); verified broader-stale >7d tier (lines 205-218); verified hook-state >7d tier includes `claude-pressure-warn-*` (line 172).
success_criteria_results.details: EXCLUDE_PRUNE shared bash array applied as `find /tmp -maxdepth 1 "${EXCLUDE_PRUNE[@]}" -o \( <preds> -print \)`. Action `-print` (dry-run) / `-print0` (real) lives INSIDE the right-hand non-prune branch — never appended after the whole expression. EXCLUDE_PRUNE covers: chrome-debug-profile, happy-attachments, claude-0, claude-commit-plan-*, happy-p05-cdp-*, *debug-profile* (defensive wildcard). /tmp/claude-0 deep .output query is the SOLE carve-out and is documented as such (lines 220-228). New patterns added to every required tier per spec.
success_criteria_results.evidence: tmp-cleanup.sh:68-75 (EXCLUDE_PRUNE), 91-115 (run_tmp), 122-141 (run_path), 144-228 (sweep categories). Dry-run executed this run: header categories all present (codex artifacts, Playwright profiles, hook state, dev/test scratch, broader stale, claude-0 deep .output, codex-outputs).

success_criteria_results.verification_method: Ran `/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh --dry-run | grep -E '^/tmp/(chrome-debug-profile|happy-attachments|claude-0|claude-commit-plan-[^/]+|happy-p05-cdp-[^/]+)$'`; confirmed exit 1 (no matches). Then `grep -c '^/tmp/claude-0/.*\.output$'` confirmed 86 carve-out candidates present. Verified hard-exclusion directories exist on disk via ls -ld.
success_criteria_results.details: 0 hard-exclusion-path candidate matches in dry-run stdout. 86 /tmp/claude-0/*.output candidates appear via the carve-out find_path query at /tmp/claude-0 (mindepth 2 -type f -name '*.output' -mtime +1).
success_criteria_results.evidence: Dry-run grep exit 1 = no hard-exclusion-path matches; /tmp/chrome-debug-profile (drwx------), /tmp/happy-attachments (drwxr-xr-x), /tmp/claude-0 (drwx------) all exist on disk; carve-out yields 86 .output candidates.

success_criteria_results.criterion: Cleanup logs report bytes freed (TOTAL across BOTH /tmp AND /var/tmp/codex-outputs) + per-category logging
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Inspected tmp-cleanup.sh:253-259 for freed_total formula; verified `numfmt --to=iec-i --suffix=B` (line 258); verified per-mount K-suffix lines preserved (lines 243-244); confirmed real-run path writes candidate paths to log via `tr '\0' '\n' < tmpfile` BEFORE rm (lines 111-112, 137-138). Checked /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log historical entries.
success_criteria_results.details: freed_total formula: `freed_total_kb = max(0, after_tmp_kb - before_tmp_kb) + max(0, after_var_kb - before_var_kb)`. Format: `numfmt --to=iec-i --suffix=B $((freed_total_kb * 1024))` → MiB/KiB/GiB suffix family. Per-category logging: real run writes candidate paths to log before rm (one path per line), satisfying AC6's 'log either candidate paths OR removed-count'. Per-mount K-suffix lines preserved unchanged. Historical log shows `freed_total=4.0KiB` confirming real-run IEC-i format works.
success_criteria_results.evidence: tmp-cleanup.sh:253-259 (formula + numfmt --to=iec-i). /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log historical entry: `freed_total=4.0KiB`. Current dry-run shows `freed_total=0B` (correct — no actual deletes). Per-category log shape verified at lines 111-112 (tr \0 \n < tmpfile BEFORE xargs rm).

root_cause_verification.rationale: Not a regression — pure enhancement. Root cause was 'natural accumulation of /tmp scratch against 4G tmpfs budget with narrow cleanup pattern coverage and no visibility/early-warning surface'. Fix addresses all 4 facets: L1 baseline visibility on SessionStart, L1.5 mid-session warning at >75%, L2 broader pattern coverage + structural correctness + truthful freed_total, L3 self-cleaning convention for future scratch.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 4 findings (this QA cycle, codex-output-546246-1779225178.txt). F1: rate-limit state on pressured filesystem (/tmp). F2: long-lived sessions can have counters swept at >7d if no pressure. F3: worst-case L1.5 runtime ~16s exceeds 15s settings timeout under both-mounts-pressured scenario. F4: -mtime +N semantics mean strictly >N full 24h periods so L3 wording is loosely accurate. Classification per codex Rule 3: F1 out_of_scope (spec explicitly mandates /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-* path; spec wins per Rule 4). F2 out_of_scope (dev report iteration-1 codex_consult already classified F3 partial-scope this way; saturated-counter case handled by mtime refresh). F3 in_scope_minor (mitigated by non-blocking design; next prompt re-emits at full fidelity). F4 out_of_scope (documentation precision improvement, not functional bug; spec uses >3d / >7d shorthand throughout). NONE blocking; verdict remains pass.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: F1, F2, F4 logged as out_of_scope_observations (spec-mandated design; spec wins). F3 logged as in_scope_minor observation. None require code changes in this cycle.

test_artifact_cleanup.blocking_reason: pretool-bash-safety.sh denies bare `rm` for QA role: 'rm is forbidden — delete files manually or ask the user'. Per Subagent Hook Discipline (CLAUDE.md), QA MUST NOT bypass hook-rejection. Test artifacts left in /tmp will be naturally swept by the next daily cron via the new `claude-pressure-warn-*` pattern at the >7d hook-state tier added in M3 of this cycle — this is by design (the L2 sweep specifically covers this exact prefix). Affected files (all under 100 bytes each): 22 test counter/lock files matching /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-qa-* and /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-codex-* and /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-held-test.lock.
test_artifact_cleanup.user_action_optional: If immediate cleanup desired, user can run: `rm /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-qa-* /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-codex-* /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-held-test.lock` from the orchestrator role (which is not subject to the QA tool-policy).
